title: Forklift Hydraulic System Repair
description: Emergency repair of hydraulic cylinder leak on forklift unit FL-042 that was causing reduced lifting capacity
body: Forklift FL-042 reported hydraulic fluid leak near left lift cylinder. Inspection revealed worn seal causing slow leak. Replaced cylinder seal kit and refilled hydraulic fluid. Tested lift capacity at full load - operating within specifications. Unit returned to service after successful test.

title: Conveyor Belt Tension Adjustment
description: Scheduled maintenance to adjust belt tension on main sortation conveyor showing signs of slippage
body: Main sortation conveyor exhibiting belt slippage during peak volume. Adjusted tension on drive rollers per manufacturer specifications. Inspected belt condition - wear within acceptable limits. Lubricated bearings and checked motor alignment. Conveyor tracking verified and adjusted. System operating normally.

title: Dock Leveler Hydraulic Service
description: Quarterly service on dock door 7 leveler including hydraulic fluid change and seal inspection
body: Performed quarterly service on dock door 7 leveler. Drained and replaced hydraulic fluid with manufacturer recommended grade. Inspected all seals - minor wear on main cylinder seal, scheduled for replacement next quarter. Tested operation through full range of motion. Lip extension and retraction working properly. Documented service in equipment log.

title: HVAC Filter Replacement
description: Monthly replacement of air handling unit filters in warehouse zone B to maintain air quality standards
body: Replaced filters on three air handling units serving warehouse zone B. Old filters showed expected particulate loading consistent with normal operations. Installed new MERV 10 filters per specification. Checked belt tension on blower motors - all within spec. Verified airflow readings at supply diffusers. System operating efficiently.

title: Emergency Lighting Test
description: Monthly functional test of emergency exit lighting and battery backup systems throughout facility
body: Conducted monthly emergency lighting test per fire code requirements. Tested all 47 emergency exit signs and 23 emergency light units. Two units in zone C showed reduced illumination - batteries replaced. All units now operating at full brightness. Test duration verified minimum 90-minute backup capability. Results documented in fire safety log.

title: Pallet Racking Inspection
description: Annual structural inspection of pallet racking systems in high-bay storage area
body: Completed annual racking inspection in high-bay area rows 15-30. Identified three uprights with minor impact damage in row 22 - scheduled for repair. All beam connectors properly engaged. Wire decking intact throughout. Load capacity placards visible and accurate. Two areas marked for additional anchoring per engineer recommendation. Full report filed with safety officer.

title: Fire Suppression System Test
description: Quarterly test of sprinkler system including flow test, alarm verification, and visual inspection
body: Performed quarterly fire suppression system test. Opened test connection - flow alarm activated within 60 seconds per code. Water pressure at test point measured 85 PSI, within acceptable range. Visual inspection of sprinkler heads showed no obstructions or damage. Fire pump started on pressure drop and ran for 10 minutes at rated capacity. System passed all tests.

title: Forklift Battery Maintenance
description: Bi-weekly battery watering and cleaning service for electric forklift fleet
body: Serviced batteries on 12 electric forklifts. Checked and adjusted water levels in all cells. Cleaned battery tops and terminals to prevent corrosion. Equalized charge on units showing cell imbalance. Two batteries showing reduced capacity - scheduled for replacement. Updated battery service tracking spreadsheet.

title: Overhead Door Motor Replacement
description: Replacement of failed motor on receiving dock door 3 that stopped responding to controls
body: Dock door 3 motor failed to respond to wall controls or remote. Troubleshooting confirmed motor failure. Removed old motor and installed replacement unit matching original specifications. Reprogrammed limits and adjusted spring tension. Tested operation through 10 full cycles - working properly. Safety sensor alignment verified.

title: Warehouse Floor Repair
description: Repair of concrete floor damage in high-traffic forklift aisle near receiving area
body: Repaired concrete spalling in aisle 5 near receiving. Area exhibited surface deterioration from forklift traffic. Applied industrial-grade epoxy patching compound to damaged sections. Feathered edges for smooth transition. Area cordoned off for 24-hour cure time. Follow-up inspection confirmed proper adhesion. Floor markings restored.

title: Scale Calibration
description: Annual calibration of floor scales in receiving and shipping areas for accurate weight measurements
body: Performed annual calibration on three floor scales - two in receiving, one in shipping. Used certified test weights from 1000 lbs to scale capacity. All scales adjusted to within 0.1% accuracy per company standard. Calibration stickers updated with current date and next due date. Certificates filed with quality department.

title: Stretch Wrapper Service
description: Quarterly service on automatic stretch wrapper including film carriage and turntable inspection
body: Serviced stretch wrapper unit SW-01. Inspected film carriage rollers and replaced worn pre-stretch roller. Cleaned photoeyes and verified height detection. Lubricated turntable bearings and checked belt tension. Tested wrap cycles on standard pallet - film tension and overlap within spec. Reset wrap cycle counter for maintenance tracking.

title: Parking Lot Light Repair
description: Emergency repair of parking lot light pole knocked over by delivery truck
body: Light pole in visitor parking struck by delivery vehicle and knocked over. Disconnected power at source and removed damaged pole. Installed replacement pole with new LED fixture. Verified proper grounding and circuit integrity. Tested light operation manually and via photocell. Damaged pole disposed of per procedures. Incident reported to safety.

title: Compressor Air Dryer Service
description: Annual service on compressed air dryer including desiccant replacement and pressure testing
body: Performed annual service on air dryer unit AD-01. Replaced desiccant beads showing saturation beyond acceptable levels. Inspected and cleaned inlet filter. Tested purge cycle timing and adjusted per manufacturer spec. Verified dewpoint at outlet - reading -40F, well within specification. System pressurized and leak checked.

title: Security Camera Cleaning
description: Quarterly cleaning and inspection of warehouse security camera system
body: Cleaned 32 security cameras throughout facility. Removed dust and debris from lenses and housings. Verified camera angles and adjusted two units that had shifted. Checked all cable connections and power supplies. Tested motion detection zones on perimeter cameras. Night vision verified functional on exterior units. Images clear on all monitors.

title: Loading Dock Bumper Replacement
description: Replacement of worn dock bumpers at doors 5 and 6 showing compression damage
body: Replaced dock bumpers at doors 5 and 6. Old bumpers showed excessive compression and cracking from heavy use. Installed heavy-duty laminated rubber bumpers rated for 50,000 cycles. Verified mounting hardware secure. Bumper height aligned with standard trailer height. Updated dock equipment inventory.
